title: Deli & Dough Uber Eats hero banner: grade + cut out four supplied deli shots, source a butcher-paper backdrop, vectorize the wordmark, and bind into an authored 2880x2304 banner template for print-ready export

source.reference: Graphic designer needed: Uber Eats banner for a NY style sandwich brand

grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Upwork brief: the shop is "gritty, bold, and a little chaotic — think New York deli meets Nonna's kitchen meets street culture. Not polished or corporate." "We need a banner for our Uber Eats page. We have the assets, they just need to be composited together so they look like they were shot in the same image. The result should feel like one cohesive, intentional photo, not a collage." The deliverable is "1 x Uber Eats banner" and "Margins must fill the expected dimension (2880 pixel width and 2304 pixel height)." REASONABLY ENRICHED for an Adobe-only, connector-doable benchmark: the raw brief implies freehand Photoshop compositing (drop layers onto a canvas by eye), which is NOT in connector scope (no from-scratch canvas layout, no generative fill, no move-tool compositing). So the client is modeled as ALSO supplying a hand-authored Illustrator banner template (banner_template.ai) at exactly 2880x2304 with named placement frames + a data-merge slug row, plus the four raw phone-shot deli photos, the flat wordmark PNG, and a backdrop-texture direction. The Adobe chain then does the genuinely-doable heavy lifting the brief actually rewards: color-grading all four photos to ONE consistent gritty-warm look so they read as "shot in the same image," cleanly lifting the hero sandwich onto transparency so it drops onto the backdrop without a hard photographic edge, sourcing/licensing a butcher-paper/deli-counter backdrop texture from Adobe Stock, vectorizing the wordmark for crisp large-scale placement, then binding every prepped part into the authored template's named frames via a genuine [T] data-merge and exporting the banner PNG/JPEG at exactly 2880x2304. The FICTIONAL brand replaces the real shop (CRUMB, @crumbsydney) with "Deli & Dough" — the intent, audience (Uber Eats storefront shoppers), and single-cohesive-banner deliverable are preserved. No trademark or proprietary data reused.
one_line_ask: Grade four raw deli photos to one cohesive gritty-warm look, cut the hero sandwich onto transparency, source a butcher-paper backdrop, vectorize the Deli & Dough wordmark, and bind all the prepped parts into the client's authored 2880x2304 Illustrator banner template to export a print-ready Uber Eats hero banner.
full_brief: Deli & Dough is a scrappy New-York-style sandwich shop in Western Sydney — gritty, bold, a little chaotic, think NY deli meets Nonna's kitchen meets street culture, not polished or corporate. We're setting up our Uber Eats storefront and need our hero banner finished. We already have the assets; they just need to be pulled together so they look like they were shot in one image — one cohesive, intentional photo, NOT a collage. Here is the hand-off: (1) an authored Adobe Illustrator banner template (banner_template.ai) built to EXACTLY 2880px wide x 2304px high (the Uber Eats hero spec), containing named placement frames — a full-bleed background frame, a hero-sandwich frame, three supporting food frames, and a wordmark frame — plus a small data-merge slug row so the tagline/handle text binds in; (2) four raw phone-shot deli photos — the hero pastrami-on-rye stacked high, a supporting Italian sub, a supporting basket of house pickles, and a supporting drip-coffee cup; (3) our wordmark as a flat cream-on-red PNG; (4) direction to back everything on a gritty butcher-paper / deli-counter texture. The four photos are inconsistent as shot: different white balance (some cool, some warm), tilted, dull and flat, busy backgrounds. The job: (a) grade all four to ONE consistent gritty-warm deli look — straighten, auto-tone, warm the white balance, lift the shadows/contrast, push vibrance a touch, and lock the look with a single shared preset so they truly read as the same shoot; crop each to the frame ratios the template expects; (b) lift the hero pastrami sandwich cleanly onto a transparent background so it drops onto the butcher-paper backdrop without a hard rectangular photo edge (that hard edge is what makes it look like a collage); (c) source and license a rustic butcher-paper/deli-counter backdrop texture from Adobe Stock; (d) vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG so it stays razor-sharp at banner size; (e) upload the prepped parts and run our authored .ai template through a data-merge so each graded photo, the cutout hero, the vector wordmark, the backdrop, and the tagline slug bind into their named frames; (f) export the bound banner to a print-ready PNG and a max-quality JPEG at EXACTLY 2880x2304. Keep it gritty and warm; do not make it clean-corporate.

inputs.name: banner_template.ai
inputs.gen_prompt: AUTHORED DELIVERABLE (cannot be model-generated): a genuine Adobe Illustrator document (.ai) sized EXACTLY 2880px x 2304px for an Uber Eats hero banner. Must contain named placement frames laid out by hand — <<bg_texture>> full-bleed background frame, <<hero_cutout>> large left-weighted frame for the transparent hero sandwich, three supporting frames <<support_1>>/<<support_2>>/<<support_3>> for the graded sub photos, a <<wordmark>> frame, and a data-merge text slug row with fields <<tagline>> and <<handle>>. Authored so real data-merge binding works; a from-scratch connector-composed canvas is NOT in scope.
inputs.realism_notes: Modeled as the client's own authored banner template — a normal freelancer hand-off. document_merge_data_vector is [T] and REQUIRES a user-authored .ai/.idml carrying real placement frames + merge fields; the connector cannot lay out a canvas from nothing, so the template is a supplied input, not a chain output. This is what makes a 'composite the supplied assets into one banner' request connector-doable.

inputs.name: photo_hero_pastrami_on_rye.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of a towering pastrami-on-rye sandwich, hand-cut thick and stacked high with steaming pink-cured meat, mustard smear and a toothpick, on a sheet of crumpled wax paper on a stainless deli counter. Slightly tilted horizon (~5 degrees), flat dull overhead lighting, slightly cool/blue white balance, a cluttered background of a busy deli pass with tickets and squeeze bottles blurred behind. Real meat texture, rye seeds, grease sheen, candid documentary look, no studio gloss.
inputs.realism_notes: Hero shot, deliberately imperfect (tilt, flat cool tone, busy background) so the grade + straighten + cutout chain has real work; gpt-image-2 for photoreal food. The tall subject gives image_remove_background a clean silhouette to lift onto transparency.

inputs.name: photo_support_italian_sub.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of an Italian sub — salami, provolone, lettuce, tomato, oil-and-vinegar on a seeded hoagie roll — cut in half on a wooden board, harsh warm tungsten cafe light, slightly overexposed on one side, warm-yellow white balance, distracting background of a napkin dispenser and menu board. Real crumb, oil sheen, no CGI gloss.
inputs.realism_notes: Supporting shot with a DIFFERENT (too-warm) white balance than the hero, so the grade must pull all four to one consistent look; justifies auto-tone + color-temperature correction.

inputs.name: photo_support_house_pickles.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of a small metal basket of house dill pickle spears with a scatter of peppercorns, on a red-and-white checked deli liner, flat grey daylight through a window, cool cast, slightly tilted, cluttered counter edge visible. Real brine sheen, dill flecks, candid feel.
inputs.realism_notes: Second supporting crop; cool + tilted + dull so it needs the same straighten/warm/tone pipeline to match the hero.

inputs.name: photo_support_drip_coffee.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal phone snapshot of a paper cup of black drip coffee with steam, a stack of sugar packets beside it, on a scratched diner counter, dim muddy warm light, slightly flat and underexposed, busy shelf of syrups blurred behind. Real cup crease, condensation, no CGI.
inputs.realism_notes: Third supporting hero; muddy/flat/underexposed tone justifies the shared auto-tone + shadow-lift + vibrance grade so all four converge on one gritty-warm look.

inputs.name: deli_and_dough_wordmark.png
inputs.gen_prompt: A flat retro deli wordmark logo reading 'DELI & DOUGH' in a bold condensed vintage signage typeface, chunky cream-white letters with a thin gold keyline, on a solid brick-red background, slight retro distress. Crisp flat raster PNG, high contrast, no gradients, suitable for vectorizing.
inputs.realism_notes: gemini-3-pro-image for clean legible text/logo; flat high-contrast so image_vectorize produces a clean razor-sharp SVG for banner-scale placement. IP-clean fictional brand mark.

inputs.name: banner_copy_slug.csv
inputs.gen_prompt: Generate a single-row CSV with columns tagline,handle holding the banner's overlay copy, e.g. tagline='STACKED HIGH. SERVED FAST.' , handle='@deliandough'. Column names must exactly match the Illustrator template merge fields <<tagline>> and <<handle>>.
inputs.realism_notes: Supplies the small amount of banner text so the [T] data-merge can bind it into the authored template's slug row; headers match the .ai merge fields so binding works. Keeps text-setting inside the authored-template lane (no headless text compositing claimed).

inputs.name: brand_direction_note.txt
inputs.gen_prompt: Write a 4-6 line client brand-direction note for Deli & Dough: gritty NY-deli / Nonna's-kitchen / street-culture aesthetic, warm and characterful NOT clean-corporate, backdrop should be rustic butcher-paper / deli-counter texture, all food photos must read as one cohesive shoot, final banner exactly 2880x2304 for Uber Eats. Include the stock-search hint 'crumpled brown butcher paper deli counter texture, warm, gritty, top-down'.
inputs.realism_notes: The written creative direction from the brief; drives the shared grade target and the Adobe Stock backdrop query. Not a pixel asset.

outputs.name: DeliAndDough_UberEats_banner_2880x2304.png
outputs.spec: Print-ready PNG at EXACTLY 2880px x 2304px (Uber Eats hero spec, margins filled), exported from the data-merged Illustrator banner: butcher-paper backdrop + cutout hero sandwich + three graded supporting photos + vector wordmark + bound tagline/handle slug, all reading as one cohesive gritty-warm image.

outputs.name: DeliAndDough_UberEats_banner_2880x2304.jpg
outputs.spec: Max-quality JPEG of the same bound banner at exactly 2880x2304, sRGB, for the Uber Eats upload flow.

outputs.name: graded_deli_photos_x4
outputs.spec: Four color-graded, straightened, template-cropped deli photos (hero pastrami / Italian sub / house pickles / drip coffee) pulled to ONE consistent gritty-warm look; the hero also delivered as a transparent-background PNG cutout.

outputs.name: deli_and_dough_wordmark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vector SVG of the Deli & Dough wordmark for razor-sharp scaling at banner size.

connector_workflow.note: Search Adobe Stock for a crumpled butcher-paper / deli-counter backdrop texture using the client's direction-note hint (low-res candidate previews + asset IDs).
License the chosen butcher-paper texture BEFORE use and download it full-res; becomes the banner background asset.
Level the ~5deg tilted horizon on the hero pastrami shot before grading.
Auto-tone the hero to fix the flat/dull exposure — the baseline for the shared look.
Warm the cool white balance toward the gritty-warm deli target the brand note calls for.
Lift the crushed dark portions so the stacked meat keeps texture — part of the shared grade.
Push vibrance a touch so the pastrami/mustard/rye read appetizing without going neon.
Apply a single shared gritty-warm preset to LOCK the cohesive look — this same preset is reused on the three supporting photos so all four match.
Crop/resize the graded hero to the aspect the template's <<hero_cutout>> frame expects.
Lift the finished hero sandwich onto a transparent background so it drops onto the butcher-paper backdrop with no hard rectangular photo edge (kills the collage look).
Run the Italian sub through the shared grade starting with auto-tone (also tames its overexposed side).
Correct the sub's too-warm tungsten white balance so it matches the hero rather than clashing — the key move to make the four read as one shoot.
Apply the SAME shared preset to the sub so it locks to the hero's gritty-warm look.
Crop the sub to the <<support_1>> frame ratio.
Straighten the tilted pickles shot.
Auto-tone the cool/dull pickles shot into the shared baseline.
Warm the cool cast on the pickles to match the hero/sub.
Apply the shared preset so the pickles lock to the cohesive look; crop follows next.
Crop the pickles to the <<support_2>> frame ratio.
Auto-tone the muddy/underexposed coffee shot to the shared baseline.
Apply the SAME shared preset so the coffee cup matches the other three — all four now read as one shoot.
Crop the coffee to the <<support_3>> frame ratio.
Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG into a clean SVG for razor-sharp placement at banner scale.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: hero_transparent_png, sub_cropped, pickles_cropped, coffee_cropped, wordmark_svg, butcherpaper_texture_fullres
connector_workflow.note: Initialize an upload session for all prepped banner parts (cutout hero, three graded supports, vector wordmark, backdrop texture).
Finalize the upload; returns the CC asset URNs the authored template's placement frames will reference.

Data-merge into the client's AUTHORED 2880x2304 .ai: bind the butcher-paper backdrop, cutout hero, three graded supports, and vector wordmark into their named frames and the tagline/handle slug from the CSV. [T] requires the user-authored template; the connector composes no new canvas — it only binds supplied parts into existing frames.
Export the merged banner to a print-ready PNG at EXACTLY 2880x2304 (margins filled per the Uber Eats spec). render_vector only EXPORTS the authored+bound document.
Export the same merged banner to a max-quality JPEG at 2880x2304 for the Uber Eats upload flow.

chaining_note: Strict output->input chaining throughout; the banner emerges only from the assembled chain. The backdrop chains asset_search(1)->license(2). The hero photo runs an 8-link grade+cutout pipeline: photo_hero_pastrami_on_rye.jpg -> auto_straighten(3) -> auto_tone(4) -> color_temperature(5) -> dark_portions(6) -> vibrance(7) -> preset(8) -> crop(9) -> remove_background(10), each step consuming only the prior step's output. The three supporting photos each feed their own grade->crop sub-chains that reuse the SAME shared preset so all four converge on one cohesive look: sub 11->12->13->14, pickles 15->16->17->18->19, coffee 20->21->22. The wordmark chains raw PNG -> vectorize(23). All prepped outputs (hero_transparent_png, sub/pickles/coffee_cropped, wordmark_svg, butcherpaper_texture_fullres) converge into upload_session(24) -> finalize(25) -> data-merge(26), which also consumes the authored banner_template.ai + copy-slug CSV. The merged .ai(26) is the single source for both exports — render PNG(27) and render JPEG(28). Nothing after the search(1) and the raw input loads originates outside the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 28 connector calls across 3 tool groups (image_* raster grade/cutout/vectorize, asset_* stock/storage, document_* Illustrator merge/render), 15 distinct Adobe tools — far above the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from the core stock_hero_expand/create challenge: taking FOUR inconsistent phone photos (different white balance, tilt, exposure) and grading them to ONE cohesive gritty-warm look via a shared preset so they read as 'shot in the same image', branching the hero into a clean transparent cutout, sourcing+licensing a backdrop texture, vectorizing a logo, then converging everything into a genuine Illustrator [T] data-merge that binds each part into named frames and dual-exporting at EXACTLY 2880x2304. It respects every hard limit: NO generative-expand/outpaint (the banner reaches 2880x2304 via the authored template's fixed canvas + render, not by outpainting pixels), NO generative fill, NO text-to-image / from-scratch art, NO freehand compositing (the connector composes no canvas — merge_data_vector/render_vector only BIND and EXPORT the client's authored .ai, listed as a supplied input), NO object-removal-with-reconstruction, NO upscaling; image_fill_area is unused; image_remove_background is used legitimately for a transparent PNG cutout; banner text is bound via the authored template's merge slug (CSV), never headless-composited.

reverify.banned_capability_check: CONFIRMED none of the banned capabilities are used. No image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / reframe-crop: the banner reaches the exact 2880x2304 target through the client's AUTHORED fixed-canvas .ai template plus document_render_vector export, never by generating/outpainting new pixels. No generative fill of new content. No text-to-image / from-scratch art or logo: the wordmark is a SUPPLIED PNG that is only vectorized (image_vectorize), and the banner layout is a SUPPLIED authored .ai, not connector-composed. No upscaling / super-resolution. No object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting: image_remove_background is used only to lift the hero onto transparency (its allowed transparent-PNG mode), reconstructing nothing behind it. No bespoke font/letterform design. No unreachable external account (Uber Eats is only the destination context, never called). image_fill_area is NOT used at all.
reverify.notes: Each potentially-risky step stays in-bounds: (1) document_merge_data_vector is [T] and REQUIRES the user-authored banner_template.ai (declared as a supplied input, kind:data, gen_model:'n/a-authored-asset') carrying real placement frames + merge fields; the connector binds supplied parts into existing frames and composes no new canvas, which is how a 'composite the supplied assets into one banner' brief becomes connector-doable without freehand compositing. (2) document_render_vector (n27/n28) only EXPORTS the authored+bound document at the template's native 2880x2304 — this is the legitimate, non-generative way to hit the exact required dimensions, replacing the banned generative-expand path other hero-expand tasks use. (3) image_remove_background (n10) -> transparent PNG only, no reconstruction. (4) banner_copy_slug.csv headers (tagline,handle) exactly match the .ai merge fields <<tagline>>/<<handle>> so the [T] bind is valid, and text is set inside the authored-template lane, not headless-composited. (5) asset_license_and_download_stock (n2) licenses BEFORE use. (6) asset_search (Adobe Stock) is distinct from the Express-widget search_design; no Express-widget or Canva tools present; all 28 steps server=='adobe'; 15 distinct Adobe tools (>=6), 28 calls (>=10) — qualifies as long-horizon flagship. SLOT-MATCH verified: category stock_hero_expand, primary op O8 / family Photo & Image, output_modality image, workflow_nature create, vertical hospitality_travel all equal the P04 slot; grounded in the 'Graphic designer needed: Uber Eats banner for a NY style sandwich brand' candidate with verbatim quotes; fictional IP-clean brand Deli & Dough replaces CRUMB/@crumbsydney.
